title: Today's AI Brief: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
summary: Coralogix raises $200M for AI monitoring, Trump's new AI order, and smart glasses for warfare

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Let's dive into today's top stories. We've got some interesting developments in the world of AI, from monitoring agents to military applications.

Coralogix, a Boston-based software monitoring startup founded in Israel, just raised $200 million in funding. They're betting that with the rise of AI agents, there's a growing need for tools to monitor and manage them. Think of it like having an air traffic controller for all the AI systems out there - someone to ensure they don't bump into each other or cause chaos. This is a pretty safe bet, considering how quickly AI is becoming a part of our daily lives.

In other news, former US President Donald Trump's new AI order has been making headlines. The order focuses on several key points, including the development and use of AI in the US. It's an interesting move, as it shows how AI is becoming a priority on the political agenda. I'm curious to see how this will impact the future of AI in the US.

Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents is also worth a quick mention.
